perturbation.original_text: "Affiliate" means, with respect to any person, any other person directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, Controlling, Controlled by or under common Control with such person and, in the case of a trust, any trustee or beneficiary (actual or potential) of that trust and, in the case of an individual, any person connected with him; provided that, for the purposes of this Agreement, neither QIWI nor any of its subsidiaries is to be regarded as an Affiliate of Otkritie
perturbation.changed_text: "Affiliate" means, with respect to any person, any other person directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, related in any way to such person. Affiliate status will be determined at the sole discretion of each party and may be revoked at any time with written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of 'Affiliate' to a much broader and subjective standard ('related in any way') and giving each party sole discretion over affiliate status. This conflicts with the rest of the agreement, which is based on a clear definition of affiliate, thus creating uncertainty when that term is being used, specifically, Clause 5.2(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of confidentiality in clause 5.1 do not apply in respect of the disclosing of Confidential Information in the following circumstances: (a) in connection with the performance of each party's obligations hereunder or the enforcement of its rights hereunder; (b) to its Affiliates and the directors, officers, employees, agents, and professional advisers or its and/or their Affiliates (the"Representatives") on a need to know basis to the extent reasonably required for purposes connected with this Agreement; 6 (c) pursuant to any listing agreement with or the rules and regulations of any recognised security exchange on which securities of suchParty or any of its Affiliates are listed and/or traded; (d) as required by Applicable Law, provided in each case set out in Clauses 5.2(a) and 5.2(b) above, the Party disclosing the same shall take all reasonable steps to preserve the confidentiality thereof and to ensure that such information shall be used only for the purposes for which it has been disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of "Affiliate" introduces a subjective and discretionary element that directly conflicts with Clause 5.2(b), which relies on a clear definition of "Affiliates" for confidentiality obligations. The change empowers each party to unilaterally determine affiliate status, making it unclear which entities are bound by the confidentiality provisions in Clause 5.2(b), creating a strong in-text contradiction, as this change introduces Ambiguity as defined in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: For two (2) years following the Effective Time, QIWI covenants that at least one half of the total transactions processed by the Contact money transfer system during such two-year period shall be settled through PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie, provided that QIWI may terminate its obligation under this Clause 4.1 at any time, in which case it shall pay to Otkritie a fee equal to the demonstrated costs incurred by Otkritie for such period starting from 1 September 2014, until the date of termination of QIWI's obligations under this Clause 4.1 in connection with (i) the transfer of settlement bank function to PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie ; and (ii) supporting the settlement bank functionality for the Contact money transfer system, provided further that such fee shall in no event exceed RUB 50 000 000 (fifty million roubles).
perturbation.changed_text: For two (2) years following the Effective Time, QIWI covenants that at least one half of the total transactions processed by the Contact money transfer system during such two-year period shall be settled through PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie. QIWI may terminate this requirement with 30 days written notice, waiving any fees or penalties.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation modifies the clause regarding QIWI's obligation to settle transactions through Otkritie's bank. The original clause allows termination of the obligation upon payment of a fee, while the changed clause allows termination with only 30 days' notice and waives any fees. This creates a direct in-text contradiction, making it unclear whether QIWI is required to pay a fee upon termination and contradicts clause 4.1 where a payment is required, leading to disputes over the termination conditions and payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For two (2) years following the Effective Time, QIWI covenants that at least one half of the total transactions processed by the Contact money transfer system during such two-year period shall be settled through PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie. QIWI may terminate this requirement with 30 days written notice, waiving any fees or penalties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that termination of the obligation to settle transactions through Otkritie requires payment of a fee. The changed text states that termination requires 30 days' notice and waives any fees or penalties. The contradicted text is the changed text, meaning it directly contradicts itself by containing conflicting terms regarding the termination of the obligation. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.
